data.executiveSummary: The frontend-web service experienced a significant performance degradation due to an N+1 query issue, negatively impacting the p95 latency.

rootCause.title: N+1 Query Problem
rootCause.description: The traces reveal an N+1 query issue where multiple sequential SELECT statements on the users_cart table were executed within a single GET /api/checkout request, causing latency degradation.

evidenceChain: Latency degradation was traced to an N+1 query problem via multiple sequential SELECT * FROM users_cart statements in a single request.
The p95 latency for the frontend-web service was increased due to this repetitive querying pattern.

timeline.event: Traces detected multiple SELECT * FROM users_cart statements within the GET /api/checkout endpoint execution.

data.impact: Users experienced slow response times during the checkout process, as the service took longer than expected to complete requests due to inefficient database querying.
recommendedActions: Implement eager loading for related data to avoid the N+1 query pattern in the GET /api/checkout endpoint.
